name: Mengyu Chang
title: Postdoctoral Fellow
institution: The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center
address: Department of Radiation Oncology, The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center, Houston, TX 77030, USA

education.institution: Department of Radiation Oncology, The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center, USA

skills: Construction and synthesis of multifunctional nanomaterials, including rare earth compounds, transition metal chalcogenides, single-atom nanozymes, metal alloys, and metallic oxides.
Surface modification of nanomaterials by noble metal deposition, in situ mineralization, and macromolecule coating.
Multimodal imaging-guided anti-tumor therapy, including photothermal, photodynamic, chemodynamic, starvation, ion-interference, ferroptotic, and immunotherapy approaches.

Cell culture, immunofluorescence, enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay, histological sectioning, small animal injection, anesthesia, blood collection, anatomy, and tissue-derived single-cell suspension preparation.
Materials characterization with XRD, SEM, TEM, UV-visible spectra, DLS, zeta potential, MSOT inVision 128 photoacoustic imaging, and flow cytometry.
awards: Prof Lee Soo Ying Young Chemist Award, Gold Award, Singapore National Institute of Chemistry, 2022
Outstanding Graduate of University of Science and Technology of China, 2021
President Award of Chinese Academy of Sciences, 2021
Excellent Doctoral Dissertation of University of Science and Technology of China, 2021
bio: Mengyu Chang is a postdoctoral fellow in the Department of Radiation Oncology at The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center.

Her research focuses on multifunctional nanomaterials for cancer diagnosis and therapy, including single-atom nanozymes, tumor microenvironment-responsive nanocomposites, multimodal imaging-guided therapy, photothermal and photodynamic therapy, ferroptosis-boosted treatment, and cancer immunotherapy.
Before joining MD Anderson, she was a postdoctoral fellow at Nanyang Technological University and completed her Ph.D. at the Changchun Institute of Applied Chemistry, Chinese Academy of Sciences.
